# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 120

This is turn 120. Turns 1 through 119 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 120.

## Turn 120 — the radical edge is a genuine uniform obstruction quotient

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit, and proof-level exponents mean the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its verifier residue. 

Turn 119 proved Fourier support on \(D_{\det}=\ker E\).  That statement strengthens to a pointwise quotient theorem.

Retain

\[
R=\Phi(K),\quad M=K/R,\quad T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[C]}M,
\quad V=M/T_0,
\]

and

\[
B=Y/R,\qquad C=Y/K,\qquad \bar B=B/T_0.
\]

Put

\[
D_R=(R^\vee)^B,\qquad
E:D_R\longrightarrow H^1(C,T_0^\vee),\qquad
D_{\det}=\ker E .
\]

Fix an exact lower map \(\rho:\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow C\). Proposition 3 identifies the dual of the \(R\)-obstruction space naturally with \(D_R\):

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(R)^\vee\simeq D_R.
\]

Choose an accessible \(V\)-lift and one \(T_0\)-lift \(\widetilde f\). Variation through the complete \(T_0\)-lift torsor defines

\[
\delta_\rho:H^1_\rho(T_0)\longrightarrow \operatorname{Ob}_\rho(R),
\qquad
[t]\longmapsto o_R(\widetilde f+t)-o_R(\widetilde f).
\tag{120.1}
\]

For every \(\lambda\in D_R\), the filtration-edge variation formula gives

\[
\big\langle\lambda,\delta_\rho([t])\big\rangle
=
\big\langle[t],\rho^*E(\lambda)\big\rangle .
\tag{120.2}
\]

Thus \(\delta_\rho\) is well-defined on cohomology, and its dual is the composite of \(E\), the injective pullback \(\rho^*\), and the perfect common-word pairing. Consequently,

\[
\ker(\delta_\rho^\vee)=\ker E=D_{\det}.
\]

Hence

\[
\operatorname{im}\delta_\rho
=
D_{\det}^{\perp}
=:\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}
\subseteq \operatorname{Ob}_\rho(R).
\tag{120.3}
\]

Under the natural dual identification, this subspace is target-defined and satisfies

\[
\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}\simeq (\operatorname{im}E)^\vee.
\]

Every element of \(\operatorname{im}\delta_\rho\) has the same number of preimages in \(H^1_\rho(T_0)\), and every cohomology class has the same number of cocycle representatives. Therefore the full \(R\)-obstruction maps each complete \(T_0\)-lift torsor uniformly onto one affine coset of \(\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}\).

Since \(T_0\leq\Phi(B)\), exact surjectivity to \(B\) is all-or-none on such a torsor. Thus, writing

\[
\mu_\Gamma(u)
=
\#\{f\in X_\Gamma(B):o_R(f)=u\},
\]

we obtain the stronger translation invariance

\[
\mu_\Gamma(u+h)=\mu_\Gamma(u)
\qquad
(h\in\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}).
\tag{120.4}
\]

Turn 119’s Fourier-support vanishing is now a corollary rather than the primary statement.

Define the residual determinant obstruction space

\[
\mathcal O_{\det,\rho}
=
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(R)/\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}.
\]

Its dual is canonically

\[
\mathcal O_{\det,\rho}^{\vee}=D_{\det}.
\tag{120.5}
\]

If \(\bar o_R\) denotes the residual obstruction, coset uniformity gives the exact factorization

\[
\#\{f\in X_\Gamma(B):o_R(f)=0\}
=
\frac{1}{|\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}|}
\#\{f\in X_\Gamma(B):\bar o_R(f)=0\}.
\tag{120.6}
\]

Fourier inversion is now performed only on \(\mathcal O_{\det,\rho}\):

\[
\begin{aligned}
\#\{f:o_R(f)=0\}
&=
\frac{1}{|\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}||D_{\det}|}
\sum_{\lambda\in D_{\det}}
\left(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)-|X_\Gamma(B)|\right)\\
&=
\frac{1}{|D_R|}
\sum_{\lambda\in D_{\det}}
\left(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}(B)-|X_\Gamma(B)|\right).
\end{aligned}
\tag{120.7}
\]

The second equality uses

\[
|D_R|=|\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad},\rho}||D_{\det}|.
\]

This explains an important normalization point in (119.8): even though the sum is restricted to \(D_{\det}\), its denominator remains \(|D_R|\), not \(|D_{\det}|\). The missing factor is exactly the uniform radical-edge quotient.

The frozen central-stage structure is therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\text{full }R\text{-obstruction}
=
\text{uniform radical-edge quotient}
+
\text{residual determinant obstruction}.
}
\]

Only the residual obstruction has characters in \(D_{\det}\). For \(0\neq\lambda\in D_{\det}\), its coefficient is computed by the inner \(D_0\)-Fourier formula and the target-derived covers \(C_{\Delta_{\lambda,a}}\to C\). Characters outside \(D_{\det}\) never enter Proposition 5 or Proposition 6; their cancellation is already built into the uniform quotient (120.6).

Thus Proposition 4 can be frozen without a zero-edge/nonzero-edge case split: first quotient by \(\mathcal O_{\mathrm{rad}}\), then apply determinant accessibility, shearing, the Gauss sum, and strictly smaller central covers to the residual obstruction. No recursive target of equal marked-kernel rank is introduced, and no modification of the candidate word is indicated.

**ESTABLISHED**: Strengthened Turn 119’s Fourier-support result to pointwise coset uniformity. Constructed the actual obstruction-variation map \(\delta_\rho\), proved that its image is \(D_{\det}^{\perp}\), and canonically factored the full \(R\)-obstruction into a uniformly distributed radical-edge quotient and a residual determinant obstruction with dual \(D_{\det}\). This also verifies the denominator in the restricted outer inversion and freezes Proposition 4 in quotient form.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the frozen six-proposition manuscript has not received an independent external line-by-line review.

**NEXT**: Replace equations (119.7)–(119.8) in the manuscript by the stronger quotient formulation (120.1)–(120.7), then submit the unchanged candidate block to the official verifier.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
